title: How Brands Grow
author: Byron Sharp
subline: What marketers don't know and what to do about it.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A brand growth chart: a smooth curve rising from bottom-left to top-right, with the curve in red. Marker dots in ash. The y-axis labeled PENETRATION, x-axis labeled TIME. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1100)" fill="none">
    <!-- Frame -->
    <line x1="-560" y1="-380" x2="-560" y2="220" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <line x1="-560" y1="220" x2="560" y2="220" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
    <!-- Y axis tick labels -->
    <text x="-580" y="-380" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#4c4c4c">100%</text>
    <text x="-580" y="-180" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#4c4c4c">50%</text>
    <text x="-580" y="220" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#4c4c4c">0%</text>
    <!-- Y axis title -->
    <text x="-620" y="-80" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#cccccc" transform="rotate(-90 -620 -80)">PENETRATION</text>
    <!-- X axis title -->
    <text x="600" y="260" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#cccccc">TIME →</text>
    <!-- Reference curves (faded) — competitor brands -->
    <path d="M-540 200 Q -300 180, 0 140 T 540 100" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.5"/>
    <path d="M-540 200 Q -300 160, 0 120 T 540 80" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.4"/>
    <path d="M-540 220 Q -300 200, 0 180 T 540 160" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.3"/>
    <!-- The brand's curve — RED, the book -->
    <path d="M-540 200 Q -300 100, 0 0 T 540 -200" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- Marker dots along the red curve -->
    <g fill="#fc1c46">
      <circle cx="-540" cy="200" r="5"/>
      <circle cx="-300" cy="100" r="5"/>
      <circle cx="-100" cy="20" r="5"/>
      <circle cx="120" cy="-40" r="5"/>
      <circle cx="320" cy="-110" r="5"/>
      <circle cx="540" cy="-200" r="7"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Top label -->
    <text x="540" y="-260" text-anchor="end" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#fc1c46">YOUR BRAND</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: A growth chart with the brand's curve in RED rising well above three faded competitor curves. The 'HOW BRANDS GROW' title in 360px uppercase weight 500 sits at the bottom. The chart is the book: penetration grows over time when you follow the laws. Single red = the winning brand's trajectory.
